research_question: How do spatial inequalities in PM2.5 exposure across California counties vary with income inequality (Gini) in 2019, and does the spatial relationship vary by urban–rural classification?
research_intent: To understand the relationship between spatial inequalities in PM2.5 exposure and income inequality across California counties and investigate how this relationship varies by urban-rural classification.

objectives.title: Assess how PM2.5 exposure varies across California counties in 2019 and whether county-level PM2.5 is spatially clustered
objectives.hypothesis: County-level annual PM2.5 exposure in 2019 is spatially clustered (positive global Moran’s I) rather than randomly distributed across California.

data_requirements.description: PM2.5 monitoring-site annual summaries to be aggregated to county-level PM2.5 exposure using `state_code`, `county_code`, `year`, and `arithmetic_mean` (filtered to PM2.5 annual standard records).

scale_note.data_scale: monitoring site points; county identifiers present

data_preprocessing.rationale: The PM2.5 data are at monitoring-site level (multiple records per county and multiple standards/metrics), but the research question requires county-level exposure for 2019; aggregating site `arithmetic_mean` to county-year produces a county exposure indicator suitable for county analysis.
data_preprocessing.method: Filter to `year == 2019` and annual PM2.5 records, then compute county-level summary (e.g., mean of site `arithmetic_mean`) by county FIPS.

data_requirements.description: County boundaries and identifiers (`GEOID`, `NAME`, `geometry`) to enable spatial weights and mapping of county-level PM2.5.

data_preprocessing.rationale: County-level PM2.5 summaries must be attached to county geometries to compute contiguity-based spatial weights and spatial autocorrelation statistics.

what_each_contributes.PM2.5_Annual_California_2019: county-level PM2.5 exposure derived from `arithmetic_mean` after aggregation
what_each_contributes.california_counties_2019: county polygon `geometry` and canonical county identifier `GEOID`

data_preprocessing.method: Create a 5-digit county FIPS key (GEOID-style) from PM (`state_code`,`county_code`) and left-join to `california_counties_2019` on `GEOID`.

steps.description: Filter `PM2.5_Annual_California_2019` to `year == 2019` and `pollutant_standard == 'PM25 Annual 2006'`, keeping `state_code`, `county_code`, `site_number`, and `arithmetic_mean`.
steps.expected_output: A 2019-only PM2.5 annual-standard table at monitoring-site record level with the columns needed for county aggregation.

steps.description: Create a 5-digit county GEOID field `GEOID` by concatenating zero-padded `state_code` (2 digits) and zero-padded `county_code` (3 digits).
Aggregate to county level by grouping on `GEOID` and computing `pm25_mean_2019 = mean(arithmetic_mean)` across monitoring sites.
steps.expected_output: A county-level PM2.5 exposure table for 2019 with one row per county GEOID.

steps.description: Left-join `pm25_county_2019.csv` to `california_counties_2019` on `GEOID` to attach `pm25_mean_2019` to county polygons.
steps.expected_output: A county polygon layer with 2019 county mean PM2.5 attached as `pm25_mean_2019`.

steps.description: Construct a Queen contiguity spatial weights structure from county `geometry` and store neighbor links keyed by `GEOID`.
Compute global Moran’s I for `pm25_mean_2019` using the Queen contiguity weights.
steps.expected_output: A table reporting Moran’s I statistic, expected I, variance, z-score, and p-value for county PM2.5 clustering in 2019.

objectives.title: Determine whether county income inequality (Gini) is associated with county PM2.5 exposure in 2019 after accounting for spatial dependence
objectives.hypothesis: Higher county income inequality (`B19083_001E:Gini Index`) is associated with higher county mean PM2.5 (`pm25_mean_2019`) in 2019, and the association remains after accounting for spatial dependence.

data_requirements.description: County-level income inequality for 2019 using `B19083_001E:Gini Index`, joined via a 5-digit county identifier derived from `state_fips` + `county_fips`.

data_preprocessing.rationale: To estimate the PM2.5–Gini relationship at the county level, Gini must be merged into the county PM2.5 geodata created in Objective 1.

what_each_contributes.California_Counties_Gini_2019: county Gini as `B19083_001E:Gini Index` plus FIPS components to form `GEOID`

data_preprocessing.method: Create `GEOID` from (`state_fips`,`county_fips`) and left-join to the county PM2.5 geodata on `GEOID`.

steps.description: Create a 5-digit county GEOID field `GEOID` by concatenating zero-padded `state_fips` (2 digits) and zero-padded `county_fips` (3 digits), and keep `GEOID`, `B19083_001E:Gini Index`, and `year`.
Left-join `gini_2019_with_geoid.csv` to `counties_pm25_2019.gpkg` on `GEOID` to attach `B19083_001E:Gini Index` to each county.
steps.expected_output: A county polygon layer containing both `pm25_mean_2019` and `B19083_001E:Gini Index` for 2019.

steps.description: Fit an OLS model with `pm25_mean_2019` as the dependent variable and `B19083_001E:Gini Index` as the predictor, and output coefficients, standard errors, and model fit statistics.
steps.expected_output: A model-results table for OLS PM2.5 ~ Gini at the county level.

steps.description: Compute global Moran’s I for OLS residuals using Queen contiguity weights keyed by `GEOID`.
steps.expected_output: A table indicating whether spatial autocorrelation remains in OLS residuals (Moran’s I, z, p).

steps.description: Fit a maximum-likelihood spatial lag model with `pm25_mean_2019` as the dependent variable and `B19083_001E:Gini Index` as the predictor using the Queen contiguity weights.
steps.expected_output: A model-results table for the spatial lag specification including coefficient estimates, spatial autoregressive parameter, and fit statistics.

objectives.title: Determine whether the PM2.5–income inequality relationship differs between urban and rural counties in California in 2019
objectives.hypothesis: The positive association between county Gini and county mean PM2.5 is stronger in metro counties (`Metro2013 == 1`) than in nonmetro counties (`Metro2013 == 0`).

data_requirements.description: County urban–rural classification using `FIPSTXT` (county FIPS including state) and the metro indicator `Metro2013` (and/or `Nonmetro2013`) for stratification and interaction testing.

scale_note.data_scale: county (58 rows), classification labeled as 2013-based codes

data_preprocessing.rationale: Urban–rural attributes must be merged into the county PM2.5+Gini geodata to test effect modification; however, the classification fields are explicitly 2013-based (e.g., `Metro2013`), so using them for 2019 is a temporal-label limitation that should be documented.
data_preprocessing.method: Format `FIPSTXT` as 5-digit string `GEOID` and left-join to the county PM2.5+Gini layer on `GEOID`.

steps.description: Create `GEOID` as a 5-character zero-padded string from `FIPSTXT` and keep `GEOID`, `Metro2013`, `Nonmetro2013`, and `RuralUrbanContinuumCode2013`.
Left-join `urban_rural_with_geoid.csv` to `counties_pm25_gini_2019.gpkg` on `GEOID` to attach `Metro2013` (and related fields) to each county.
steps.expected_output: A county polygon layer with `pm25_mean_2019`, `B19083_001E:Gini Index`, and `Metro2013` available for interaction and stratified analyses.

steps.description: Create an interaction term `gini_x_metro = (B19083_001E:Gini Index) * Metro2013`.
Fit an OLS model with `pm25_mean_2019` as the dependent variable and predictors `B19083_001E:Gini Index`, `Metro2013`, and `gini_x_metro`, and output coefficient estimates and p-values.
steps.expected_output: A model-results table testing whether the PM2.5–Gini slope differs by metro status (interaction term significance and direction).

steps.description: Compute Pearson correlation between `pm25_mean_2019` and `B19083_001E:Gini Index` separately for counties where `Metro2013 == 1` and where `Metro2013 == 0`.
steps.expected_output: A two-row summary table of within-group correlations (metro vs nonmetro) with sample sizes.
